talk_metadata.talk_title: Spectrum of RAS alleles across cancer using AACR Project Genie
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "I don't have that data, but obviously that would be very important to know and very interesting, especially in the context of treatment"
Speaker said: "You're right, we're limited in what we can analyze for based on... I looked at it in terms of common drivers and then the targets that made sense"

key_data_gaps: No treatment response or resistance data
No pre‑ vs post‑therapy allele distribution

unresolved_questions: How do RAS allele frequencies differ between pre‑treatment and post‑resistance samples?
What sample size is needed to detect mutual exclusivity for low‑frequency co‑mutations?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Neomorphic p53 alleles drive organ‑specific metastatic programs
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "I don't think there is a definitive answer for that. But I would say the conformational changes are more likely to be involved in the loss of function"
Speaker said: "We are interested in focusing, especially on hotspot p53 mutations and how they are driving unique mechanistic programs in different metastatic organ sites"

key_data_gaps: Limited clinical outcome data linking specific p53 mutants to therapy response
No functional validation of CSF1‑dependent macrophage recruitment in vivo

unresolved_questions: Do different p53 mutant classes (DNA‑contact vs conformational) alter chromatin deposition differently?
Can CSF1‑dependent macrophage signaling be therapeutically targeted in mutant‑p53 metastasis?

talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: Dana‑Farber Cancer Institute / Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center
talk_metadata.talk_title: KMT2C mutations predict platinum sensitivity across solid tumors
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We saw that KMT2C deletions and loss‑of‑function mutations were more prevalent than other canonical HRD mutations like BRCA1/2"
Speaker said: "Patients with KMT2C mutation had a mean overall survival of 51 months compared to 25.3 months in the wild type"

trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Broad inclusion of all solid tumors without prospective biomarker stratification
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Prospective KMT2C testing at treatment decision
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Potential confounding by tumor type and line of therapy

key_data_gaps: No prospective randomized trial confirming KMT2C as predictive biomarker
Limited exploration of HRD signatures beyond BRCA/PALB2

crispro_opportunity.description: KMT2C mutation status could be used to prospectively select colorectal cancer patients for platinum‑based regimens where no biomarker currently exists.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "KMT2C occurs in about 8% of colorectal cancer, so this could be meaningful"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate KMT2C mutation data with HRD signature modeling to refine patient selection for platinum therapy.

crispro_opportunity.description: Existing PARP inhibitors could be repurposed for KMT2C‑mutant tumors given the HRD‑like phenotype.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "If KMT2C loss creates functional HRD deficiency, then patients harboring KMT2C mutation should have increased sensitivity to platinum… and possibly PARP inhibition"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict synthetic lethal interactions between KMT2C loss and PARP inhibition across tumor types.

unresolved_questions: Does high tumor mutation burden in KMT2C‑mutant patients confound the observed platinum benefit?
What is the mechanistic link between KMT2C loss and homologous recombination deficiency?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Rare BRAF class II alterations in pancreatic cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Class II, including N486K/P190 alteration and fusion, are more frequent in pancreatic cancer"
Speaker said: "Class II tends to have worse overall survival than class I"

key_data_gaps: Lack of outcome data linking class II BRAF alterations to response to specific inhibitors
Assay heterogeneity between Japanese FoundationOne and GENIE panels

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop MEK inhibitor‑based combos targeting class II BRAF dimer drivers in pancreatic cancer.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "Class II BRAF alterations, especially inflammatory, are mainly seen in pancreatic cancer, highlighting therapeutic potential of MEK inhibitors or novel BRAF dimer‑targeting agents"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model downstream MAPK signaling dependencies of class II BRAF mutants to prioritize MEK inhibitor combinations.

unresolved_questions: Are the observed class II prevalence differences driven by biology or by assay detection limits?
What is the optimal therapeutic strategy for N486K/P190 pancreatic tumors?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Ultima Genomics duplex whole‑genome cfDNA for clonal evolution tracking
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We are able to reconstruct phylogenetic trees with the PPMseq data and see higher concordance compared to whole‑exome"
Speaker said: "Ultima's assay is able to recover more duplex molecules, giving higher effective depth than comparable Illumina approaches"

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Ultima Genomics PPMseq duplex whole‑genome cfDNA assay
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed that the assay relies on proprietary flow‑based sequencing and duplex chemistry, suggesting limited external replication without licensing
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Current clinical adoption limited to research collaborations; no FDA‑cleared indication yet

key_data_gaps: Limited number of patients (6) with matched tissue and cfDNA

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO can leverage the high‑resolution clone tracking from Ultima PPMseq to improve predictive modeling of resistance emergence.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "With whole genome sequencing we can be more confident in the tree and identify driver mutations on unique branches"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Integrate duplex‑derived clonal fractions into CrisPRO's evolutionary simulations for earlier detection of emergent resistant subclones.

unresolved_questions: What is the minimal duplex depth required for reliable detection of low‑frequency subclones in routine clinical cfDNA?
Can structural variant detection from cfDNA improve therapeutic decision‑making beyond SNV tracking?

talk_metadata.talk_title: AI‑integrated gut microbiome analysis in early vs late onset colorectal cancer among Hispanic/Latino patients
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Overall, when we look at early onset, we do have a lowering in microbial diversity for this early onset group pretty significantly"
Speaker said: "Bacteroides is highly enriched in the early onset... Veillonella is also highly enriched"

key_data_gaps: Very small fecal cohort (n=23) limits generalizability

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify microbial signatures (e.g., Bacteroides, Veillonella) that could serve as early‑onset CRC risk markers in Hispanic/Latino populations.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "Bacteroides is highly enriched in the early onset... Veillonella is also highly enriched"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate microbial abundance data with host genomic and clinical features to build multi‑modal risk prediction models.

crispro_opportunity.description: Design prospective trials stratifying patients by microbiome diversity and KMT2C/KRAS status to test targeted prevention strategies.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted the need for larger comparative studies to better understand risks for this high‑risk population.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO to simulate trial power when incorporating microbiome‑derived covariates.

unresolved_questions: How does BMI interact with specific microbial taxa to influence early‑onset CRC risk?
Will larger, longitudinal cohorts confirm the observed diversity reductions?
